title: Never share your OTP
tip: Your OTP (One Time Password) is like the key to your bank locker. No bank, government officer, or helpline will EVER ask for it. If someone asks — it is a scam. Hang up immediately.

title: Two-step verification
tip: Go to WhatsApp → Settings → Account → Two-step verification → Enable. This adds a 6-digit PIN that protects your account even if someone gets your SIM card.

title: Receiving money needs no PIN
tip: You only enter your UPI PIN when SENDING money. If someone says 'Enter your PIN to receive ₹5000', that is fraud — they are actually withdrawing from your account.

title: Use a passphrase
tip: Instead of a hard-to-remember password like 'P@ss123', try a phrase you love: 'MyGardenHas12Roses!' — long, easy to remember, very hard to crack.

title: Check sender email carefully
tip: Scammers copy logos of real banks and companies. Before clicking any link, check the full email address — 'support@sbi-secure.com' is fake; only 'support@sbi.co.in' is real.

title: QR codes can be dangerous
tip: Never scan a QR code sent by a stranger. Fake QR codes can open your UPI app and create a payment request that drains your account — always verify who sent it.

title: Look for the lock icon
tip: Before entering any password or bank details on a website, look for a small padlock (🔒) before the website address. No padlock means the website is not secure.

title: Truecaller helps identify scams
tip: Install Truecaller on your phone. It shows the name and 'Scam Likely' warning for known fraud numbers before you even answer the call.

title: What are cookies?
tip: When websites ask 'Accept cookies?' — these are small files that remember your preferences but also track what you do online. You can always say 'Reject' or 'Only necessary' to share less.

title: Enable SMS alerts for all transactions
tip: Register your mobile number with your bank and enable SMS alerts for every transaction — even ₹1. This way you immediately know if any unauthorized payment happens.

title: Verify before you share
tip: Before forwarding any news or message on WhatsApp, check if it is true on factcheck.in or vishvasnews.com. Sharing false news can hurt people you care about.

title: UPI Collect request fraud
tip: If you get a payment 'collect' request from an unknown person with a reason like 'lottery' or 'prize', REJECT it. They are trying to take money FROM you, not send it to you.

title: Book doctor appointments online
tip: Apps like Practo, Apollo 247, and government's eSanjeevani let you consult real doctors from home — no travel, no waiting. Ask a family member to help you set it up once.

title: Ola/Uber are safe for seniors
tip: Both apps show your driver's name, photo, car number, and a live trip tracker that you can share with family. Your family can watch your trip in real time for extra safety.

title: Cash on delivery is safer
tip: When shopping on Amazon or Flipkart for the first time, choose Cash on Delivery. You pay only after seeing and accepting the product — no risk of losing money online.
